steps.text: Write the perimeter formula for a rectangle: $P = 2(\text{length} + \text{width})$. With length = 55 and width = $x$: $P = 2(55 + x) = 110 + 2x$.
"At most 150 inches" means the perimeter is less than or equal to 150, so the inequality is $2x + 110 \leq 150$.

Evaluate all choices:
  • A: $2x + 110 \leq 150$ — matches the perimeter expression with the correct ≤ sign for "at most." That confirms A is correct.
  • B: $2x + 110 \geq 150$ — ≥ means "at least," not "at most." Eliminated.
  • C: $x + 55 \leq 150$ — omits the factor of 2; this is only half the perimeter. Eliminated.
  • D: $x + 55 \geq 150$ — wrong sign and wrong perimeter expression. Eliminated.
